title: Hydrocotyle hookeri
intro: Hydrocotyle hookeri, also known as Indian pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a creeping plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Araliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects, contributing to the improvement of cognitive function, memory, and overall health. The bioactive constituents of Hydrocotyle hookeri include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and glycosides, which have been found to possess anti-stress and anti-anxiety properties. In traditional Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine, Hydrocotyle hookeri is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, capsules, and tablets, as well as topical creams and ointments, to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and support skin health.
taxonomy: Hydrocotyle hookeri is a species of plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a flowering plant native to Australia, commonly found in freshwater environments. The classification of Hydrocotyle hookeri includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. The classification also includes its class as Magnoliopsida and its order as Apiales. Hydrocotyle hookeri is further classified into the family Araliaceae, subfamily Hydrocotyloideae, and the genus Hydrocotyle.
